titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/244

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=216 status=live modified=2017-03-30 15:49:56.000215+00 | translation moa=217 status=live modified=2017-03-30 15:50:06.203911+00

div.p:
. W . dei gratia Rex Scott’. Omnibus probis hominibus Totius terre sue Clericis ⁊ laicis Salutem. Sciant
presentes ⁊ futuri me concessisse . ⁊ hac Carta mea confirmasse deo ⁊ Ecclesie Sancte crucis
de Edeneburg . ⁊ Canonicis ibidem deo seruiebtibus . donationem illam quam Gilebertus de vmfran
villa eis fecit . de una Carukata terre in kinard’ . sicud eis per probos homines
patrie perambulata fuit . cum communi pastura sicut in Carta predicti . G . continentur
Et cum libertate multure sue ⁊ hominum suorum super terram illam sedentium  sicut in carta
predicti . G . continentur. Tenend’ in liberam ⁊ quietam ⁊ perpetuam elemoysinam . Ita libere ⁊ quiete .
plenarie . ⁊ honorifice . sicut alias terras ⁊ tenuras suas tenent ⁊ possident . ⁊
sicut Carta prediti . G . testatur . saluo seruicio meo . Testibus . Hug’ Canceallario meo . Roberto
Capellano meo Ioc’ archidiacono Dunk’ . Ricard’ de prebenda clericis meis . Philippo de valon’ .
Walter de berch’ Camerario . Ricardo filio hugonis . Willelmo de Rameseia . Adam de Sireis apud Edeneburg .

William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting: Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood at Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there, the donation that Gilbert de Umfraville made to them, of one ploughgate of land in Kinnaird, just as it was perambulated by the worthy men of the country, with common pasture as contained in the charter of the said Gilbert, and with freedom of multure and of their own men staying on their land, as contained in the charter of the said Gilbert. To be held in free and quiet and perpetual alms, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably as they hold and possess their other lands and holdings, as the charter of the said Gilbert bears witness. Reserving my service. As witnesses, Hugh my chancellor, Robert my chaplain, Jocelin the archdeacon of Dunkeld, Richard de Prebenda my clerks, Philip de Valognes, Walter de Berkeley the chamberlain, Richard son of Hugh, William de Ramsay, Adam of Ceres. At Edinburgh.
